---
title: "System Overview"
guide-section: "Architecture"
---

# System Overview

Tenant First Aid provides legal information about housing and eviction in Oregon.
It uses a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architecture so that answers are
grounded in the actual text of Oregon housing law rather than the model's prior.
LangChain abstracts the model and agent orchestration.

The application is a Flask-based Python backend serving a React frontend,
deployed on Digital Ocean.

::: {#fig-system-architecture}
```{mermaid}
graph TB
    subgraph "Client"
        Frontend[React Frontend<br/>Vite + TypeScript]
    end

    subgraph "Backend Services"
        API[Flask API Server<br/>Python]
    end

    subgraph "AI/ML Services"
        subgraph "LangChain"
            Gemini[Google Gemini 2.5 Pro<br/>LLM]
            RAG[Vertex AI RAG<br/>Document Retrieval]
            Corpus[RAG Corpus<br/>Oregon Housing Law]
        end
    end

    subgraph "Infrastructure"
        Nginx[Nginx<br/>Reverse Proxy]
        DO[Digital Ocean<br/>Ubuntu 24.04 LTS]
        Systemd[Systemd Service<br/>Process Management]
    end

    Frontend --> API
    API --> Gemini
    Gemini --> RAG
    RAG --> Corpus
    Nginx --> API
    DO --> Nginx
    Systemd --> API

    style Frontend fill:#61dafb
    style API fill:#0d47a1
    style Gemini fill:#4285f4
    style RAG fill:#4285f4
```

High-level system architecture — client, backend, AI/ML services, and infrastructure.
:::

## Key components

- **Frontend** — React 19 + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind CSS.
- **Backend** — Flask API with LangChain agent orchestration.
- **LLM** — Google Gemini 2.5 Pro via Vertex AI.
- **Retrieval** — Vertex AI RAG (Vertex AI Search) with metadata filtering for
  jurisdiction-specific responses.
- **Infrastructure** — Digital Ocean Droplet (Ubuntu 24.04) behind an Nginx
  reverse proxy, managed by systemd.

## Request lifecycle

1. The user asks a question in the React frontend.
2. The question is sent to the Flask API (`POST /api/query`).
3. Flask hands the message and location context to the LangChain agent.
4. The agent decides to call the RAG retrieval tool.
5. Vertex AI RAG searches the document corpus, filtered by the user's city/state.
6. Retrieved passages are returned to Gemini as context.
7. Gemini generates a response that cites the relevant statutes.
8. The response streams back to the frontend chunk by chunk.
9. The frontend renders the answer in real time.

## Where to go next

- To orient in the code, read [Backend Overview](02-backend-overview.qmd).
- To understand how retrieval actually works, jump to
  [RAG & Document Retrieval](03-rag-and-retrieval.qmd).
